title: orthocarpus lithospermoides var. bicolor
intro: Orthocarpus lithospermoides var. bicolor, also known as the two-colored woodland myrtle or bicolor woodland myrtle, is a perennial herb that belongs to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Orthocarpus lithospermoides var. bicolor include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
